title: Fresh Chewy Algerian White Fish Tagine
description: A romantic Algerian dish featuring fresh white fish fillets cooked in a flavorful tagine with a rich deglaze, perfect for a special occasion. This halal-friendly recipe serves 4 people.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the white fish fillets and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are cooked through and flake easily with a fork.
Remove the fish from the tagine and set it aside on a plate.
Add the chicken broth to the tagine and bring it to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the broth for 5 minutes, or until it has reduced slightly and the flavors have melded together.
Return the fish to the tagine and spoon some of the sauce over the top of each fillet.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve immediately.
